adds.rationale: Fills the profile's empty quantum bucket with a US-listed name that has been public since late 2021 and shows accelerating commercial revenue and government contract traction heading into Q2-25.

news_evidence.summary: IonQ closed its de-SPAC merger and began trading on NYSE in October 2021, giving it a multi-year price history available as of the rebalance date.

Quantum computing stocks ran sharply higher in late 2024 on optimism over commercial milestones, then sold off in early 2025 to more reasonable entry levels - a buildup-phase pattern for a wave the profile names as a likely next wave.

removes.rationale: Heavy thematic overlap with NVDA (its top holding) plus fresh tariff-risk catalyst (Trump's Feb 18 announcement of 25%+ semiconductor tariffs) and the DeepSeek-driven Jan 27 semi-complex selloff suggest the AI bucket should hold single-name NVDA rather than double up via a broad semi ETF.

news_evidence.summary: Nvidia lost ~$589B in market cap on Jan 27, 2025 (largest single-day loss in US history, -17%), with the semi complex selling off in sympathy after DeepSeek R1's release; SMH as a diversified semi ETF absorbed the full sector hit.
Trump announced plans on Feb 18, 2025 for 25%-and-higher tariffs on imported semiconductors, creating direct policy risk for broad semi ETFs.

rationale_overall: AI bucket goes from 4 to 3 (removing the SMH/NVDA double-count after DeepSeek and tariff shocks compressed broad-semi risk-reward), and the quantum bucket opens at 1 via IONQ.
